api: Svix REST API v1 (https://api.svix.com/api/v1; OpenAPI 3.1 spec published at https://api.svix.com/api/v1/openapi.json)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the real published OpenAPI spec (117 operations across /api/v1, plus a separate /ingest/api/v1 surface). Covered: the core webhook-sending management surface an outbound-webhooks-as-a-service account actually operates on day to day -- applications, endpoints, event types, outgoing messages, background tasks, connectors (transformation templates), and operational webhook endpoints (account-level ops events) -- as 7 read streams and 16 write actions. Excluded, by category: message-attempt/delivery-log detail (nested 2-level fan-out the engine's single-level fan_out dialect cannot express -- requires_elevated_scope), endpoint secret/header/transformation/stats sub-resources and bulk-replay/recover/send-example/precheck operational actions (requires_elevated_scope or non_data_endpoint), the newer Stream/Poller/Ingest-source beta product surface (out_of_scope -- a distinct, separately-versioned product line Svix ships alongside classic outgoing webhooks), auth/portal-access/session endpoints (non_data_endpoint), environment import/export (binary_payload), and single-object detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape (duplicate_of).

excluded.reason: single-application detail; identical record shape to the applications list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

partial-update variant of the same application resource update_application (PUT) already covers; this dialect's write actions send a full-body replace, which is a strict superset of what a PATCH caller needs

message-attempt log for one endpoint, nested two levels deep (app_id then endpoint_id) -- the engine's fan_out dialect resolves exactly one level of parent ids per stream; a second nested fan-out level is not expressible in Tier 1
message-attempt log for one message, reachable via the endpoint-nested attempt listing above; same nested-fan-out gap, and the underlying attempt records are the same shape either way
single-endpoint detail; identical record shape to the endpoints list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
partial-update variant of the same endpoint resource update_endpoint (PUT) already covers
asynchronous bulk message-redelivery job kickoff for a date range, not a synchronous single-record create/update/delete this dialect's write model expresses
custom HTTP header sub-object of the endpoint resource, embedded in/updatable alongside the endpoint itself; not modeled as a separate list-shaped read stream

sets custom outgoing HTTP headers (may include sensitive values, e.g. downstream auth tokens) on an endpoint; a narrow sub-resource mutation better scoped to a dedicated future action than folded into update_endpoint's general-purpose body
same custom-header sub-resource as the PUT variant above
attempted-messages-for-one-endpoint list, nested two levels deep (app_id then endpoint_id) -- the same nested-fan-out gap as the attempt log above
asynchronous bulk redelivery-of-failed-messages job kickoff, not a synchronous record mutation
asynchronous bulk gap-fill redelivery job kickoff, not a synchronous record mutation
returns the endpoint's HMAC signing secret; a credential-shaped value that should never flow through an ordinary read stream into destination-warehouse record data
rotates the endpoint's HMAC signing secret; a credential-lifecycle action, not an ordinary data mutation, and immediately invalidates the previous secret for every downstream signature verifier
sends a synthetic test webhook event for a chosen event type, not a real business message; a debugging aid, not a data mutation
delivery-success/failure count aggregate over a time window, not a syncable object list
per-endpoint inline payload-transformation JS sub-object, embedded in/updatable via the endpoint resource; not modeled as a separate read stream
sets the per-endpoint inline transformation JS; a narrow sub-resource mutation better scoped to a dedicated future action
app-portal-access integration key management; a portal/auth-adjacent feature, not core webhook-sending data, out of scope for this pass
same app-portal integration feature as the list endpoint above
same app-portal integration feature
same app-portal integration feature
same app-portal integration feature
returns an app-portal integration's secret key; credential-shaped value
rotates an app-portal integration's secret key; credential-lifecycle action
irreversibly scrubs the persisted payload content of every message on the application (GDPR-style erasure), never a reverse-ETL-safe mutation
dry-run check of whether a payload would match any active endpoint filter, not a data read or mutation
single-message detail; identical record shape to the messages list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single delivery-attempt detail, nested three levels deep (app_id, msg_id, attempt_id); same nested-fan-out gap as the attempt log endpoints above
irreversibly scrubs one delivery attempt's persisted response content; a targeted erasure action, not a reverse-ETL-safe mutation
irreversibly scrubs one message's persisted payload content; a targeted erasure action, not a reverse-ETL-safe mutation
per-endpoint delivery status for one message, nested two levels deep (app_id then msg_id); same nested-fan-out gap as the attempt log endpoints
manually re-triggers delivery of one already-sent message to one endpoint; an operational retry action, not an ordinary data mutation
Poller/Sink beta product surface, a distinct, separately-versioned feature line from classic outgoing webhooks; out of scope for this pass
Poller/Sink beta product surface
Poller/Sink beta product surface
mints a scoped dashboard-portal access token; a session/auth action, not a data operation
expires every outstanding app-portal access token for the application; a session-revocation admin action
ends the caller's own dashboard session; a session action, never a data operation
Stream beta product's session logout; out of scope (non_data_endpoint takes precedence -- it is a session action regardless of product surface)
Stream beta product surface, a distinct, separately-versioned feature line; out of scope for this pass
Stream beta product surface

Stream beta product surface
single-background-task detail; identical record shape to the background_tasks list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-connector detail; identical record shape to the connectors list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
partial-update variant of the same connector resource update_connector (PUT) already covers
exports the entire environment (all apps/endpoints/event types/connectors) as a single opaque export-file blob, not a per-record data operation
imports an opaque environment export-file blob, replacing/merging configuration in bulk; not a per-record data operation
bulk-generates event type definitions from an uploaded OpenAPI document; a document-processing bulk-import action, not a single-record create this dialect's write model expresses
single-event-type detail; identical record shape to the event_types list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
partial-update variant of the same event-type resource update_event_type (PUT) already covers
service liveness probe, not application data
single-operational-webhook-endpoint detail; identical record shape to the operational_webhook_endpoints list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
custom HTTP header sub-object of the operational webhook endpoint resource; not modeled as a separate read stream
sets custom outgoing HTTP headers on an operational webhook endpoint; a narrow sub-resource mutation better scoped to a dedicated future action
returns the operational webhook endpoint's HMAC signing secret; a credential-shaped value
rotates the operational webhook endpoint's HMAC signing secret; a credential-lifecycle action
per-application usage/billing aggregate, not a syncable object list
event-type usage aggregate configuration, not a syncable object list
